danger_signals.visual_form: Maskers redesigned as futuristic cyborgs with mechanical features
danger_signals.meaning: Evolution from terrorist threat to technological/alien danger; elevated threat level from original game

danger_signals.visual_form: Bullets and laser projectiles from enemies
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate death on contact; single hit kills player

danger_signals.visual_form: Masker cyborgs positioned throughout stage environments
danger_signals.meaning: Active combat zones; player cannot proceed safely without engagement

danger_signals.visual_form: Satellite destruction, base camouflage across global locations
danger_signals.meaning: Worldwide threat level; enemy organization is active and dangerous

safety_signals.visual_form: Marked doors with ammunition counter display
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zones where players can restock weapons and coordinate resources

safety_signals.visual_form: Submachine gun (Walther MP-based) available via marked doors
safety_signals.meaning: Increased player power; progression through danger

safety_signals.visual_form: Stage progression from Florida resort to Egyptian pyramid
safety_signals.meaning: Defined safe endpoint; movement forward is progress toward victory

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Rolling Thunder organization emblem (implied from title and narrative)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Ammo storage rooms, secure corridors within camouflaged bases

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Masker cyborgs (primary foot soldiers); futuristic mechanical design
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Seaside resort in Florida (stage 1-4); pyramid structure in Egypt (stage 5-8); both locations are camouflaged Geldra bases

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Futuristic graphics, cyborg enemies, modern technology aesthetic, satellite destruction plots
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: More aggressive enemy AI; cyborg enemies demonstrate higher threat capability than previous hooded terrorists

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Multiple international stage locations (Florida, Egypt); visible satellite destruction
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Continuous enemy patrols; resistance encountered across diverse environments

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Limited ammunition display; weapons degrade to slow 'chaser' bullets when depleted
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Players must use marked doors to resupply; ammo does not carry between levels; strategic resource management required

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Female agent Leila (Player 1) and male agent Albatross (Player 2) with different external appearances and distinct handguns
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Cooperative gameplay; both agents can leave ammunition for each other in marked rooms

environmental_storytelling.example: Seaside resort in Florida and pyramid in Egypt presented as tourist/historical sites but revealed to be Geldra bases; narrative and visuals expose hidden threat infrastructure
Maskers transformed from hooded terrorists to cyborgs; visual shift communicates escalation of threat technology and indicates series progression

environmental_storytelling.visual_form: Ammunition counter in marked doors; limited pistol rounds at level start
environmental_storytelling.storytelling_purpose: Conveys player vulnerability and need for tactical cooperation between agents

environmental_storytelling.example: Shared ammo pools in marked doors where one player can leave ammunition for the other; architecture and mechanics reflect teamwork necessity

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Pursue players through corridors; fire projectiles (bullets/lasers) causing immediate death on contact
Can absorb two physical hits before death; single bullet/projectile hit results in death
Fires normal bullets; when depleted, fires slow 'chaser' bullets as fallback
Higher damage output; better crowd control capability against Masker cyborgs

notes: Rolling Thunder 2 uses visual and mechanical communication to establish a 1990s espionage-action world with elevated technological threat. The shift from hooded terrorists to cyborgs signals narrative evolution. Cooperative ammo-sharing mechanics and weapon degradation create visual/mechanical expression of vulnerability and teamwork necessity. Stage locations (camouflaged bases) use environmental reveal as storytelling tool. Character gender dimorphism (Leila/Albatross) with identical mechanics represents 1991-era approach to female protagonist inclusion.
sources_used: wikipedia - Rolling Thunder 2 main article (plot, gameplay, character details, settings, weapon systems, enemy design evolution)
